summary: Backend pytest (30/30 passed) covering health, PG listing/filters/detail, auth (register/login/me/google failure), bookings (create/list/error cases), mock Razorpay payments, reviews, owner CRUD with ownership 403, admin endpoints with role enforcement. Frontend e2e Playwright passed for: home featured cards, /pgs.html filters & search results, /register.html -> /dashboard.html, end-to-end booking with mock payment dialog -> booking marked 'paid' in dashboard, admin login -> /admin.html stats & tabs, owner login -> /owner.html tabs, /list-pg.html form render with default room row. CRITICAL setup bug found & fixed: serve was started with -s (single-page) flag, causing every non-index *.html page to be redirected to / and served as index.html (clean-URLs SPA fallback). All non-home frontend pages were completely inaccessible until fix.

ui_bugs.issue: PG card images on the search results page render as gray placeholders even though the same Unsplash URLs render fine on the home page Featured PGs section. Likely a CSS background-image / aspect ratio container issue in pg-result-card template.

action_items: Confirm the serve.json + removal of -s flag (committed by tester) is acceptable; otherwise pages like /pgs.html /login.html /register.html /admin.html /owner.html /dashboard.html /list-pg.html /pg-detail.html are all inaccessible (return home page content).
Investigate why /pgs.html PG result cards show gray image placeholders while the same image URLs work on home page Featured cards (CSS issue in pg-result-card template).
Optional: seed PG data has limited results for Bengaluru + price 4000-7000 filter (only 2 results). Not a bug, just FYI.
critical_code_review_comments: Frontend serve config: package.json used 'serve -s public' which enabled cleanUrls + SPA fallback. With cleanUrls, /pgs.html was 301-redirected to /pgs and then the SPA fallback served index.html. FIXED by removing -s and adding /app/frontend/public/serve.json with {cleanUrls:false, trailingSlash:false}.
server.py: PG status auto-set to 'pending' for owner-created PGs is correct, and update_pg correctly strips 'status' field from non-admin updates (verified by tests).
Booking endpoint computes total = monthly_rent*duration_months + deposit and stores it in DB. Verified via test_booking_created.
Razorpay mock mode correctly returns is_mock=true and accepts any signature when RAZORPAY_KEY_ID == 'rzp_test_placeholder'.
CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False is fine since frontend uses Bearer header (not cookies). No issue.
All endpoints serialize MongoDB ObjectId via serialize() helper - _id is correctly stripped and exposed as 'id'.
server.py is ~862 lines and would benefit from being split into routers (auth, pgs, bookings, payments, reviews, admin) for maintainability - not blocking, just hygiene.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all major flows pass; only PG card image rendering on /pgs.html is broken)

test_credentials: Admin admin@stayhive.com / Admin@123 (verified working). Owner owner@stayhive.com / Owner@123 (verified working). Guests created at runtime as e2eb_<ts>@test.com / Test@123.
seed_data_creation: Backend lifespan auto-seeded admin, demo owner, and 6 sample PGs. Tests created additional guests and one PG (cleaned up via owner-delete) and one E2E paid booking.
context_for_next_testing_agent: If /pgs.html or other pages return home content, check that /app/frontend/public/serve.json exists with {cleanUrls:false} and that package.json's start script does NOT include the -s flag. Backend mock payment flow: frontend dashboard.payNow() and pg-detail.book-submit use window.confirm() to simulate Razorpay; Playwright must register page.on('dialog', d => d.accept()) BEFORE clicking pay.
rca_of_critical_issue: Root cause: The frontend was started with `serve -s public`. The `-s/--single` flag turns serve into SPA-mode (rewrites unknown paths to index.html) AND keeps cleanUrls=true which strips .html extensions via 301. Browser requested /pgs.html -> 301 /pgs -> file /pgs not found -> SPA fallback returned index.html with content-disposition: filename='index.html'. Reproduction: curl -sI <BASE>/pgs.html returned 301 to /pgs, and curl <BASE>/pgs returned home page HTML. Mitigation: removed -s flag from start script and added serve.json with cleanUrls:false, then restarted frontend via supervisor.
